mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, FLT3 (including ITD and resistant mutants), and FGFR kinases
additional_notes: Approved for CML/Ph+ ALL; potent against FLT3-ITD; activity reported in MOLM-13/MOLM-14/MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, PDGFR, and VEGFR kinases
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/GIST; AML growth inhibition via FLT3 blockade
Approved for CRC/GIST/HCC; reported AML cell activity

mechanism: Targets VEGFR/PDGFR with some FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/STS; preclinical AML activity

mechanism: Broad tyrosine kinase blockade including SRC, ABL, and KIT
additional_notes: Approved for CML/Ph+ ALL; activity variable in MOLM-13; sub- to low-micromolar in AML lines

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits 26S proteasome leading to proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for MM/MCL; low-nanomolar activity in MOLM-13 and other AML lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the 20S proteasome chymotrypsin-like activity
additional_notes: Approved for MM; tens of nanomolar potency in AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial protein synthesis reducing OXPHOS
additional_notes: ~1–5 µM in MOLM-13 and primary AML cells; targets leukemic stem/OXPHOS dependence

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase inducing oxidative stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Active in FLT3-ITD AML including MOLM-13

mechanism: Generates reactive oxygen species causing cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Low-micromolar activity in MOLM-13 and other AML lines; synergy with other agents reported

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial function; inhibits Wnt/STAT3 signaling
additional_notes: Sub- to low-micromolar activity in AML; MOLM-13 included in some studies

mechanism: Disrupts microtubule polymerization causing mitotic stress
additional_notes: Sub- to low-micromolar growth inhibition in AML lines

mechanism: Dopamine D2 receptor antagonism; reported activity against leukemia stem/progenitor cells
additional_notes: Low-micromolar AML activity; cardiotoxicity/QT prolongation is a concern

mechanism: Modulates Kv1.3 potassium channels and mitochondrial ETC
additional_notes: Sub- to low-micromolar activity in AML models
Approved for MF/PV; single-agent low-micromolar in AML; synergy with FLT3/MEK inhibitors reported

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce cap-dependent translation and proliferation
additional_notes: Pathway inhibition at low-nanomolar; AML viability effects often higher and assay-dependent

mechanism: Inhibits ribosomal elongation to suppress global translation
additional_notes: Also known as homoharringtonine; approved for CML; strong activity in AML lines

mechanism: Functional inhibitor of eIF4E-mediated cap-dependent translation
additional_notes: Low-micromolar activity in eIF4E-dependent AML; early clinical signals in AML
